Getting help with sceptre

Source: SUPPORT.md

Questions and usage support

For questions about using sceptre, interpreting outputs, or choosing an analysis workflow, start a GitHub Q&A discussion. This keeps answers discoverable for future users. If your question genuinely cannot be made public because it contains confidential information, you may email Eugene Katsevich at ekatsevi@wharton.upenn.edu. Please use email only when a public Q&A discussion is not possible; whenever feasible, remove sensitive details and ask the question publicly instead.

Bugs

Report reproducible bugs through GitHub Issues. A useful bug report includes:

  • the installed sceptre version and installation source;
  • the R version and operating system;
  • a minimal reproducible example using simulated or public data;
  • the exact error or unexpected result; and
  • the expected behavior.

Feature requests

Propose new capabilities in the Feature requests discussion category. Describe the problem or user need, the desired user-facing behavior, relevant workarounds or alternatives, and how the capability could be validated. Maintainers may promote a sufficiently scoped proposal to an issue for implementation tracking.

Conduct concerns

  • For a Code of Conduct concern, follow CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md.

Do not include private participant data, credentials, protected health information, or other sensitive material in public support channels.

On this page

Developed by Timothy Barry, Louis Deutsch, Eugene Katsevich, Wharton Analytics, National Science Foundation.
